title: show_chat_list() extraction co-locates optimistic state update with send()
description: show_chat_list() is extracted from update() as a method on App. The extraction is not purely cosmetic: the method holds both the tx.send(AppCommand::SelectChat) and the optimistic local state mutations (selected_chat, messages.clear(), message_bodies.clear(), message_timestamps.clear()) in a single code block. Keeping these together is a correctness property — the UI must clear the stale message list at the same moment it sends the SelectChat command, so the render loop never shows messages from the previously selected chat while the background task fetches new ones. Splitting this into separate sites would risk the clear and the send drifting apart. A future contributor might inline show_chat_list() back into update() to reduce indirection — that is acceptable only if the four co-located mutations remain adjacent to the send.
close_reason: Good decision already implemented in current codebase
